results.name: PNA__Implementation_Plan_2025_WFL1
results.title: PNA Implementation Plan 2025 Map Viewer
results.description:
Overview

This hosted feature layer supports the Los Angeles County Park Needs Assessment Plus (PNA+) Implementation Plan 2025. It provides spatial data related to park need, disadvantaged communities, environmental conditions, land opportunities, and coalition-identified projects. The dataset is intended to inform analysis, visualization, and decision-making around equitable park investment and implementation priorities.

Content

This layer contains features derived from county, regional, state, and private data sources that have been processed by MIG, Inc. for integration with the PNA+ Implementation Plan. Related datasets include:

  • Focus Areas: Disadvantaged Communities & Park Need
  • Vacant Lands
  • Tax-Defaulted Parcels
  • Public K–12 Schools in Park Deficit Areas
  • Repairs to Poor-Condition Parks
  • Coalition Priority Projects
  • Trails and Bikeways
  • Biodiversity
  • LUST Cleanup Sites
  • Wildfire Perimeters 2025

 

All data have been clipped, filtered, and symbolized for relevance to the PNA+ Implementation Focus Areas.

Data Notes

This dataset represents a processed and integrated version of multiple source datasets and may not reflect the most recent authoritative data maintained by the original providers. For the most current or authoritative data, refer to the source agencies cited in the layer’s metadata.

Credits

Data are derived from county, regional, state, and private sources, including RPOSD, SCAG, CDFW, DTSC, OEHHA, and UrbanFootprint, Inc.
results.snippet: Spatial dataset supporting the Los Angeles County Park Needs Assessment Plus (PNA+) Implementation Plan 2025, representing park need, environmental conditions, and opportunity areas for equitable park investment.
results.licenseInfo: This dataset is provided for informational and planning purposes only and should not be used for regulatory or legal determinations. All data have been processed to support analysis and visualization of countywide patterns and project opportunities under the PNA+ Implementation Plan.

results.name: PNA_Plus_Map_Layers
results.title: Park Needs Assessment Plus - GIS Layers
results.description:
On December 6, 2022, the Los Angeles County Board of Supervisors (BOS) adopted the 2022 Countywide Parks Needs Assessment Plus (PNA+) Final Report. Consistent with this Board action, DPR is making GIS data from the PNA+ available to the public here. Composite layers include:

Regional Study Areas

Rural Study Areas

Regional Site Inventory

Local Parks

Beaches

Countywide Trails

Trailheads and Access Points

Priority Areas for Increasing Access to Regional Recreation

Priority Areas for Increasing Access to Rural Recreation

Priority Area for Environmental Restoration

Environmental Benefits

Environmental Burdens

Composite Population Vulnerability

Note that all data sources in the web map are courtesy of the Los Angeles County Department of Parks and Recreation (DPR). If you'd like to learn more about the data and analysis used in the PNA+, visit https://lacountyparkneeds.org/pnaplus-report/. 

DISCLAIMER: The data herein is for informational purposes, and may not have been prepared for or be suitable for legal, engineering, or surveying intents. The County of Los Angeles reserves the right to change, restrict, or discontinue access at any time. All users of the maps and data presented on https://lacounty.maps.arcgis.com or deriving from any LA County REST URLs agree to the "Terms of Use" outlined on the County of LA Enterprise GIS (eGIS) Hub (https://egis-lacounty.hub.arcgis.com/pages/terms-of-use).

results.title: PNA Implementation Plan 2025 Map Viewer
results.description:
Overview

This web map supports the Los Angeles County Park Needs Assessment Plus (PNA+) Implementation Plan 2025. It provides an integrated, countywide view of spatial data related to park need, disadvantaged communities, environmental conditions, land opportunities, and coalition-identified projects. The map is intended for exploration and visualization to inform equitable park investment, regional coordination, and data-driven implementation priorities.

Content

The web map includes layers derived from multiple county, regional, state, and private data sources that have been processed by MIG, Inc. for integration with the PNA+ Implementation Plan. Layers symbolize and spatially represent topics such as:

  • Focus Areas: Disadvantaged Communities & Park Need
  • Vacant Lands
  • Tax-Defaulted Parcels
  • Public K–12 Schools in Park Deficit Areas
  • Repairs to Poor-Condition Parks
  • Coalition Priority Projects
  • Trails and Bikeways
  • Biodiversity
  • LUST Cleanup Sites
  • Wildfire Perimeters 2025

 

All layers have been clipped, filtered, and symbolized for relevance to the PNA+ Implementation Focus Areas and designed for visual clarity in the ArcGIS Online Map Viewer.

Data Notes

This map references derived datasets that may not reflect the most recent authoritative versions maintained by the original data providers. For the most current data, refer to the source agencies cited in each layer’s metadata. Symbology and display scales are optimized for visualization and may generalize data geometry or attributes.

Credits

Data sources include the Los Angeles County Department of Parks and Recreation (RPOSD), Southern California Association of Governments (SCAG), California Department of Fish and Wildlife (CDFW), Department of Toxic Substances Control (DTSC), Office of Environmental Health Hazard Assessment (OEHHA), and UrbanFootprint, Inc. Map compiled and symbolized by MIG, Inc. for the Los Angeles County Park Needs Assessment Plus (PNA+) Implementation Plan 2025.
results.snippet: Interactive web map supporting the Los Angeles County Park Needs Assessment Plus (PNA+) Implementation Plan 2025, designed to visualize park need, environmental conditions, and opportunity areas that guide equitable park investment and implementation across Los Angeles County.
results.licenseInfo: This map is provided for informational and planning purposes only and should not be used for regulatory, legal, or engineering determinations. All data have been processed for visualizing countywide patterns and identifying project opportunities under the PNA+ Implementation Plan.

results.name: LA_County_ParkNeedsAssessment_2015
results.title: LA_County_ParkNeedsAssessment_2015
results.description: In March 2015, the Los Angeles County Board of Supervisors approved a motion to initiate the Countywide Comprehensive Parks and Recreation Needs Assessment.  This represents an unprecedented effort to document existing parks and recreation facilities in cities and unincorporated communities in Los Angeles County, and to use these data to determine the scope, scale, and location of park need in the County. The inventory and analysis of parks and open space that was completed during the course of the Parks Needs Assessment generated many maps and new datasets. These maps and data are now available to the public for download. For additional information about the Parks Needs Assessment, please visit the project website at www.lacountyparkneeds.org
results.snippet: LA County Comprehensive Parks and Recreation Needs Assessment: this feature class shows study area boundaries and the park need framework score they have been given on a scale of 1 - 5 [5=Very High Need; 4=High Need; 3=Moderate; 2=Low; 1=Very Low].
results.accessInformation: Los Angeles County Parks and Recreation 2015

results.title: LA City Park Needs Assessment
results.description:
This is the full set of GIS data in relation to the City of Los Angeles Park Needs Assessment that was formalized in 2025. The five included layers are:

  1. PNAParkPixels - Representational technique for City of Los Angeles Department of Recreation and Parks park sites within the "Universe of Sites". 
  2. PNAParkPixels_PerSquareMile - Representational technique for City of Los Angeles Department of Recreation and Parks New Park Priority Areas. This was created as a part of the 2025 Park Needs Assessment to represent the "Universe of Sites".
  3. PNAParkPixels_Regions - Accompanies the Park Pixels which are a representational technique for City of Los Angeles Department of Recreation and Parks park sites within the "Universe of Sites". This layer can be used as an overlay to delineate the Park Pixels by their region.  This was created as a part of the 2025 Park Needs Assessment.
  4. PNAParks - Inventory of City of Los Angeles Department of Recreation and Parks park sites, updated 12/01/2025 for the Park Needs Assessment.
  5. PNAPrioritizationScores - Prioritization of City of Los Angeles Department of Recreation and Parks park sites and 36 identified New Park Priority areas, updated 12/01/2025 for the Park Needs Assessment.

More information including the full park needs assessment report can be found at https://needs.parks.lacity.gov/
results.snippet: An assortment of layers related to the 2025 Park Needs Assessment in the City of Los Angeles.
results.licenseInfo: The data contained within is for informational purposes only.

results.name: ParkNeed_Detailed
results.title: Park Needs Assessment Detailed (Hosted - Public)
results.description:
In March 2015, the Los Angeles County Board of Supervisors approved a motion to initiate the Countywide Comprehensive Parks and Recreation Needs Assessment.  This represents an unprecedented effort to document existing parks and recreation facilities in cities and unincorporated communities and to use these data to determine the scope, scale, and location of park need in Los Angeles County.
The Parks Needs Assessment will help local officials, park agencies, and residents understand the future steps that need to be taken to ensure all communities have adequate access to thriving parks. 

This feature layer takes a closer look at park need in detail.  To locate the overall study area results polygon layer, click here.

results.title: Schools and County Parks
results.description: An application showing all public schools in Los Angeles County and their relationship with County Parks.  A walkable network was used to show schools that are within a 10-minute walk of County Parks.  A look at Park Need and its relationship to key health data factors are also explored here.

results.title: PNA Interactive Map Viewer
results.description:
The Park Needs Assessment (PNA) Application is a public-facing GIS platform developed by the Los Angeles County Department of Parks and Recreation to support ongoing efforts in planning equitable park and recreation investments. Building on the original 2016 PNA, this updated tool incorporates new data layers and analytical tools that reflect key themes including:

  • Environmental resilience and nature-based solutions
  • Park access and acreage gaps
  • Community vulnerability and demographic indicators
  • Health, equity, and environmental burden
  • Opportunities for habitat restoration and urban greening

Users can interact with mapped data across various geographies—including Study Areas, Census Tracts, and Supervisorial Districts—while also accessing the latest composite indices and project opportunity layers. This tool supports community-based organizations, public agencies, and policy makers in prioritizing funding and resources where the need is greatest.

The app was developed to increase transparency, engagement, and collaboration around park equity and Measure A implementation. It is part of the County’s long-term strategy to promote healthy, climate-resilient communities through expanded access to quality park space and recreation infrastructure.
results.snippet: Display of relevant layers from the 2016 Parks Needs Assessment (PNA) report, designed for public access at the Los Angeles County Department of Parks and Recreation.
results.licenseInfo: The data herein is for informational purposes and may not have been prepared for or be suitable for legal, engineering, or surveying intents. The County of Los Angeles reserves the right to change, restrict or discontinue access at any time. All users of the maps and data presented on https://lacounty.maps.arcgis.com or deriving from any LA County REST URLs, agree to the following Terms of Use as outlined on the County of LA Enterprise GIS (eGIS) Hub (https://egislacounty.hub.arcgis.com/pages/terms-of-use).

results.name: PNA2016_Park_0_5mi_Walkshed
results.title: PNA2016_Park_0_5mi_Walkshed
results.description: This feature class displays areas within a half-mile walking distance of reported parks by study area. This was created using the the Walkability Network Dataset for LA County.
results.snippet: This layer represents the areas within a half-mile walkshed of parks in Los Angeles County, as originally calculated for Figure 32 in the 2016 Los Angeles Countywide Comprehensive Parks & Recreation Needs Assessment. It reflects access to parks based on park locations and pedestrian network conditions as of 2016.

results.title: Park Need Assessment Map
results.description: Park Need Study Area information, including the level of need, demographics, and health indicators.   Park need levels were determined as part of our Comprehensive Countywide Park Needs Assessment, completed in 2016.  All demographics are current as of June, 2020.  Health indicators are from CalEnviroScreen's study from 2018.

results.title: Los Angeles County Park Needs
results.description:
This dataset assesses the need for parks in Los Angeles County by city as part of the Countywide Comprehensive Parks and Recreation Needs Assessment. It documents existing parks and recreation facilities in cities and unincorporated communities to determine the scope, scale, and location of park need. Information like this may be useful for studying equitable access to greenspace across communities.

Spatial Extent: Los Angeles County

Spatial Unit: Cities

Created: 2016

Updated: as needed

Source: Department of Parks and Recreation

Contact Telephone:

Contact Email: jdiaz2@parks.lacounty.gov

Source Link: https://lacountyparkneeds.org/
